sanskrit: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः । तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसंगः समाचर ॥
hindiTranslation: यज्ञ कर्तव्यपालन के लिये किये जानेवाले कर्मोंसे अन्यत्र अपने लिये किये जानेवाले कर्मोंमें लगा हुआ यह मनुष्यसमुदाय कर्मोंसे बँधता है, इसलिये हे कुन्तीनन्दन  तू आसक्तिरहित होकर उस यज्ञके लिये ही कर्तव्यकर्म कर ॥
englishTranslation: Actions done for sacrifice free a person; actions done for personal gain bind. Therefore, Arjuna, do your duty without attachment for that purpose alone.
essence: Work becomes bondage when it is done for yourself.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Work done only for personal gain creates bondage. Work done as an offering, without attachment, should be done instead.
simpleInsight: There is relief in serving something larger than your own gain.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before battle. Krishna sharpens the lesson: action itself is not the problem. Action done for selfish gain binds; action offered without grasping becomes the right way to move.
modernRelevance: You send the email, then keep checking for praise. The knot tightens when the action is for your ego; it loosens when the action is for what needs doing.
translationLiteral: यज्ञार्थात् (for the purpose of sacrifice) / कर्मणः (from action) / अन्यत्र (other than) / लोकः अयम् (this world) / कर्मबन्धनः (bound by action) / तत्-अर्थम् (for that purpose) / कर्म (action) / कौन्तेय (son of Kunti) / मुक्तसंगः (free from attachment) / समाचर (perform)
insight: It shifts effort from personal payoff to clean participation in duty.

quoteVariants.text: Do your duty for the whole. Self-serving work binds you.
Work for the whole, not yourself.
The work binds only when it serves me.
